gloss.en: to use power or influence to affect something

words.example: He exerted all his authority to make them accept the plan.

gloss.en: being sent away from your own country

words.example: He spent ten years in exile.

gloss.en: a way out of a building or vehicle

words.example: Where's the exit?

gloss.en: the act of spending money; money spent

words.example: The government plans to reduce military expenditure.

gloss.en: based on new, untested ideas or methods

words.example: The school uses experimental teaching methods.

gloss.en: to no longer be valid after a set time

words.example: When does your driving licence expire?

gloss.en: clear and easy to understand, leaving no doubt

words.example: He gave me very explicit directions on how to get there.

gloss.en: clearly and directly, with no doubt

words.example: The report states explicitly that the system was to blame.

gloss.en: treating people unfairly to make money

words.example: The law protects children from exploitation.

gloss.en: able or likely to explode

words.example: The police found an explosive device in the car.

gloss.en: a short passage from a book, film, etc.

words.example: The following extract is taken from her new novel.

gloss.en: a person with extreme political or religious views

words.example: The attack was carried out by political extremists.

gloss.en: to make an action or process easier

words.example: The new trade agreement should facilitate economic growth.

gloss.en: a small group within a larger one with different aims

words.example: There are rival factions within the administration.

gloss.en: a department or group of departments in a university

words.example: She studies at the Faculty of Law.

gloss.en: to become paler or less bright

words.example: The curtains had faded in the sun.

gloss.en: the quality of treating people equally and reasonably

words.example: The fairness of the judicial system is being questioned.

gloss.en: causing or ending in death

words.example: He was killed in a fatal accident.

gloss.en: what will happen or has happened to someone

words.example: The fate of the three men is unknown.

gloss.en: giving people a good opinion of something

words.example: She made a favourable impression on his parents.

gloss.en: an action needing skill, strength or courage

words.example: The tunnel is a remarkable feat of engineering.

gloss.en: based on the belief in equal rights for women

words.example: She wrote a book about feminist theories.

gloss.en: the part of food that keeps the bowels healthy

words.example: You should eat more dietary fibre.

gloss.en: angry, aggressive and frightening

words.example: They keep a fierce dog to guard the house.

gloss.en: a person who makes films or movies

words.example: The film-maker spent two years shooting the movie.

gloss.en: a device that removes unwanted material from liquid or gas

words.example: You should change the air filter regularly.

gloss.en: in good health

words.example: ‘How are you?’ ‘Fine, thanks.’

gloss.en: a gun that can be carried

words.example: The police were issued with firearms.

gloss.en: healthy and strong from regular exercise

words.example: Top athletes have to be very fit.

gloss.en: a sports event arranged for a certain date and place

words.example: There are plans to make the race an annual fixture.

gloss.en: a mistake that makes something not work correctly

words.example: The argument is full of fundamental flaws.

gloss.en: having a flaw; not perfect or correct

words.example: The plan was seriously flawed.

gloss.en: to leave a place quickly because of danger

words.example: She burst into tears and fled.

gloss.en: a group of military ships under one command

words.example: The navy sent a fleet of destroyers.

gloss.en: the soft part of the body between skin and bones

words.example: The trap had cut deeply into the rabbit's flesh.

gloss.en: the ability to change to suit new situations

words.example: The new system offers a greater degree of flexibility.

gloss.en: to develop quickly and become successful

words.example: Few businesses are flourishing in the present economy.

gloss.en: able to flow like a liquid

words.example: The doctor checked his body fluids.

gloss.en: part of a film showing a particular event

words.example: They showed old film footage of the moon landing.

gloss.en: a person from a different country

words.example: Being a foreigner was a big disadvantage.

gloss.en: to work hard to build something strong and lasting

words.example: They want to forge new links between management and workers.

gloss.en: letters or symbols that represent a rule or law

words.example: This formula is used to calculate the area of a circle.

gloss.en: to create or prepare something carefully and in detail

words.example: It took months to formulate a new policy.

gloss.en: away from a place; out

words.example: They set forth at dawn.

gloss.en: going to happen or appear very soon

words.example: Everyone is talking about the forthcoming elections.

gloss.en: to encourage something to develop

words.example: The club aims to foster better relations in the community.

gloss.en: easily broken or damaged

words.example: Be careful with the fragile glass.

gloss.en: permission to sell a company's goods in an area

words.example: They signed a franchise agreement with the company.

gloss.en: in an honest and direct way

words.example: He spoke frankly about the ordeal.

gloss.en: annoyed because you cannot do what you want

words.example: It's very easy to get frustrated in this job.

gloss.en: making you feel annoyed and impatient
gloss.zh: 令人沮丧的；令人懊恼的

words.example: It's frustrating to have to wait so long.

gloss.en: the feeling of being annoyed and impatient

words.example: Dave thumped the table in frustration.

gloss.en: practical and useful, with little decoration

words.example: Bathrooms don't have to be purely functional.

gloss.en: collecting money for a charity or organization

words.example: The hospice is planning a major fundraising event.

gloss.en: a ceremony for burying or cremating a dead person

words.example: Hundreds of people attended the funeral.

gloss.en: a unit for measuring liquid (about 3.8–4.5 litres)

words.example: The tankers carried thousands of gallons of fuel.

gloss.en: playing games of chance for money; betting

words.example: Online gambling has become very popular.

gloss.en: a meeting of people for a particular purpose

words.example: We held a small family gathering.

gloss.en: a long, steady look at someone or something

words.example: He met her gaze and smiled.

gloss.en: part in a vehicle that changes engine to wheel speed

words.example: Careless use of the clutch may damage the gears.

gloss.en: shared by a whole group; not specific

words.example: ‘Vine fruit’ is the generic term for currants and raisins.

gloss.en: the mass murder of a nation or ethnic group

words.example: Refugees gave accounts of the mass genocide.

gloss.en: a quick look

words.example: She took a quick glance at the newspaper headlines.

gloss.en: a brief, incomplete sight of someone or something

words.example: He caught a glimpse of her in the crowd.

gloss.en: bringing great success, fame and praise

words.example: We congratulate you on this glorious victory.

gloss.en: great success that brings praise and fame

words.example: She won Olympic glory in the 100 metres.

gloss.en: the way a country or organization is controlled

words.example: He stressed the company's standards of good governance.

gloss.en: smooth, attractive and controlled movement

words.example: She moves with the natural grace of a ballerina.

gloss.en: a firm hold of or control over something

words.example: I grabbed him, but he slipped from my grasp.

gloss.en: very serious and giving cause for worry

words.example: The police have expressed grave concern about the child.

gloss.en: the force that pulls objects towards the earth

words.example: He explained Newton's law of gravity.

gloss.en: a pattern of crossing lines forming squares

words.example: New York has a famous grid of streets.

gloss.en: a very sad feeling, especially after a death

words.example: She was overcome with grief when her husband died.

gloss.en: a wide smile

words.example: She gave a broad grin.

gloss.en: to crush something into small pieces

words.example: She likes to grind coffee fresh each morning.

gloss.en: an act of holding something tightly

words.example: Keep a tight grip on the rope.

gloss.en: being the total amount before anything is taken away

words.example: The gross weight includes the packaging.

gloss.en: a member of a small, unofficial fighting group

words.example: The town was attacked by urban guerrillas.

gloss.en: help or advice given by someone experienced

words.example: Teachers need guidance on how to use video in class.

gloss.en: unhappy feeling from doing something wrong

words.example: She had feelings of guilt about leaving her children.

gloss.en: the tube in the body that food passes through

words.example: Food can take many hours to pass through the gut.

gloss.en: to describe someone or something as very good

words.example: The conference was hailed as a great success.

gloss.en: at an equal distance between two points

words.example: It's about halfway between London and Bristol.

gloss.en: an act of stopping movement or progress

words.example: Work came to a halt when the machine broke down.

gloss.en: the amount that can be held in one hand

words.example: She threw a handful of rice into the pot.

gloss.en: the way someone deals with a situation

words.example: I was impressed by his handling of the affair.

gloss.en: easy and convenient to use

words.example: This is a handy little tool.

gloss.en: annoying or threatening someone with pressure

words.example: The company has clear rules against sexual harassment.

gloss.en: the physical machines and parts of a computer

words.example: We supply computer hardware to businesses.

gloss.en: a state of peaceful existence and agreement

words.example: They worked together in racial harmony.

gloss.en: cruel, severe and unkind

words.example: The punishment was harsh and unfair.

gloss.en: the time when crops are gathered; the gathering

words.example: The farmers are busy at harvest time.

gloss.en: a very strong feeling of dislike

words.example: He looked at me with intense hatred.

gloss.en: if a ghost haunts a place, it appears there

words.example: People say a headless rider haunts these lanes.

gloss.en: something that can be dangerous or cause harm

words.example: The broken wire is a serious safety hazard.

gloss.en: to make a feeling or effect stronger

words.example: Tension has heightened after the recent attack.

gloss.en: a country's traditions, buildings and history

words.example: Spain has a rich cultural heritage.

gloss.en: a system ranking people by importance

words.example: She rose quickly through the social hierarchy.

gloss.en: getting a lot of attention in the media

words.example: They launched a high-profile campaign.

gloss.en: an indirect suggestion of what you mean

words.example: He gave a broad hint that he was thinking of retiring.

gloss.en: the country where a person was born

words.example: Many refugees were forced to flee their homeland.

gloss.en: a curved piece of metal for hanging things on

words.example: Hang your coat on the hook by the door.

gloss.en: believing that what you want will happen

words.example: I feel hopeful that we'll find a house soon.

gloss.en: the line where the sky seems to meet the land

words.example: The sun sank below the horizon.

gloss.en: a hard pointed part on some animals' heads

words.example: The bull had two large curved horns.

gloss.en: a person held prisoner to force others to act

words.example: Three children were taken hostage in the robbery.

gloss.en: aggressive, unfriendly and ready to fight

words.example: The speaker got a hostile reception from the audience.

gloss.en: aggressive or unfriendly feelings or behaviour

words.example: There was barely veiled hostility in her tone.

gloss.en: connected with helping people who suffer

words.example: They provided humanitarian aid to the war zone.

gloss.en: people in general

words.example: He was found guilty of crimes against humanity.

gloss.en: not thinking you are better than others

words.example: Be humble enough to learn from your mistakes.

gloss.en: a light, colourless gas, the lightest chemical element

words.example: Water is made of hydrogen and oxygen.

gloss.en: the act of recognizing or proving who someone is

words.example: The identification of the crash victims took weeks.

gloss.en: based on or connected with an ideology

words.example: The two parties have deep ideological differences.

gloss.en: a set of ideas an economic or political system is based on

words.example: The party follows a Marxist ideology.

gloss.en: a rude word for a very stupid person

words.example: When I lost my passport, I felt such an idiot.

gloss.en: a lack of knowledge or information

words.example: They fought a long battle against prejudice and ignorance.

gloss.en: language that creates pictures in the mind

words.example: The poem is full of vivid poetic imagery.

gloss.en: extremely large or great

words.example: There is still an immense amount of work to be done.

gloss.en: likely to happen very soon

words.example: They warned of the imminent threat of invasion.
